assertions.text: A car pulled out in front of me on the fast road.
I was traveling around 110 km/h before braking.
My speed was reduced to walking pace within a few seconds after braking.
The emergency braking occurred after the other car pulled out in front of me.
There was no contact — we never touched.
Once clear (after avoiding collision), I accelerated and picked up speed again.

verdicts.evidence: The claim that "A car pulled out in front of me" is about another road user's action and is not recorded by the vehicle sensors; no external object-detection channels are available so this is [unverifiable] (no numeric citation).

Telemetry shows a sustained deceleration event on device axis accel_x from [t=0.22 to t=4.11](T3) with a mean magnitude of [0.55 g](T3) and an integrated device-frame delta-V of [−51.8 km/h](T2), indicating an emergency braking maneuver consistent with "I emergency-braked."
GPS speed samples show the first recorded speed at [113.0 km/h at t=0.0](T2), which supports the statement of traveling around [110 km/h](T2) before braking.

GPS speed fell from [113.0 km/h at t=0.0] to [8.0 km/h minimum over the window](T2), with intermediate values showing speeds of [42.0 km/h at t=15.0](T2); the detect_events summary records speed drops including a [from 26.0 to 10.0 km/h between t=6.0 and t=8.0](T3), supporting that speed was reduced to walking pace within a few seconds after braking.

Sequence timing between "other car pulled out" and the braking is about relative road-user actions; sensors record only vehicle motion. While braking is detected ([t=0.22 to t=4.11](T3)), there is no sensor channel indicating the other vehicle's maneuver, so the assertion is [unverifiable].

Crash/impact detectors show no high-g impact spikes (no impact-level accel >2.5 g detected) and detect_events did not list any CISS impact; accel peaks are below impact thresholds (peak_abs_dev on accel_x [0.777 g at t=1.12](T2)), consistent with "no contact — we never touched."

After the braking interval, GPS speed rises again within the window: first value [113.0 km/h at t=0.0] falls then shows later values up to a max of [113.0 km/h] and last_value [42.0 km/h at t=15.0], and detect_events shows multiple speed drops then higher speeds earlier in the window; the telemetry shows recovery and nonzero forward speed after the avoidance, supporting that the driver accelerated and picked up speed again.

result.rationale: Sensor data show a clear, sustained deceleration starting near [t=0.22 to t=4.11](T3) with GPS speed beginning at [113.0 km/h](T2), large integrated delta-V of [−51.8 km/h](T2), no impact-level accel spikes (peak [0.777 g at t=1.12](T2)), and subsequent nonzero speeds; therefore the narrative is overall consistent with the telemetry.

citations.claim: from 26.0 to 10.0 km/h between t=6.0 and t=8.0

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_z.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

detections.note: direction ambiguous: device axis orientation undocumented

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
